title: TCR-EML: Explainable Model Layers for TCR-pMHC Prediction
content: T cell receptor (TCR) recognition of peptide-MHC (pMHC) complexes is a central component of adaptive immunity, with implications for vaccine design, cancer immunotherapy, and autoimmune disease. While recent advances in machine learning have improved prediction of TCR-pMHC binding, the most effective approaches are black-box transformer models that cannot provide a rationale for predictions. Post-hoc explanation methods can provide insight with respect to the input but do not explicitly model biochemical mechanisms (e.g. known binding regions), as in TCR-pMHC binding. "Explain-by-design" models (i.e., with architectural components that can be examined directly after training) have been explored in other domains, but have not been used for TCR-pMHC binding. We propose explainable model layers (TCR-EML) that can be incorporated into protein-language model backbones for TCR-pMHC modeling. Our approach uses prototype layers for amino acid residue contacts drawn from known TCR-pMHC binding mechanisms, enabling high-quality explanations for predicted TCR-pMHC binding. Experiments of our proposed method on large-scale datasets demonstrate competitive predictive accuracy and generalization, and evaluation on the TCR-XAI benchmark demonstrates improved explainability compared with existing approaches. Explainable Model Layers (TCR-EML) include a Feature Enhancement and Fusion (FEF) block and contact prototype layers, which not only predict TCR-pMHC binding but also provide contact scores corresponding to contact distances. In the absence of experimental TCR-pMHC structures, the contact prototype illuminates TCR-pMHC binding patterns. TCR-EML: Explainable Model Layers for TCR-pMHC Prediction Learning Meaningful Representations of Life (LMRL) Workshop at ICLR 2026 LMRL Jiarui Li Zixiang Yin Zhengming Ding Samuel J. Landry Ramgopal R. Mettu
desc: Learning Meaningful Representations of Life (LMRL) Workshop at ICLR 2026

title: Quantifying Cross-Attention Interaction in Transformers for Interpreting TCR-pMHC Binding
content: CD8+ killer T cells and CD4+ helper T cells play a central role in the adaptive immune system by recognizing antigens presented by Major Histocompatibility Complex (pMHC) molecules via T Cell Receptors (TCRs). Modeling binding between T cells and the pMHC complex is fundamental to understanding basic mechanisms of human immune response as well as in developing therapies. While transformer-based models such as TULIP have achieved impressive performance in this domain, their black-box nature precludes interpretability and thus limits a deeper mechanistic understanding of T cell response. Most existing post-hoc explainable AI (XAI) methods are confined to encoder-only, co-attention, or model-specific architectures and cannot handle encoder-decoder transformers used in TCR-pMHC modeling. To address this gap, we propose Quantifying Cross-Attention Interaction (QCAI), a new post-hoc method designed to interpret the cross-attention mechanisms in transformer decoders. Quantitative evaluation is a challenge for XAI methods; we have compiled TCR-XAI, a benchmark consisting of 274 experimentally determined TCR-pMHC structures to serve as ground truth for binding. Using these structures we compute physical distances between relevant amino acid residues in the TCR-pMHC interaction region and evaluate how well our method and others estimate the importance of residues in this region across the dataset. We show that QCAI achieves state-of-the-art performance on both interpretability and prediction accuracy under the TCR-XAI benchmark. Quantifying Cross-Attention Interaction (QCAI) is a post-hoc explanation method designed for cross-attention mechanisms. In this paper, we show that QCAI enables insight into the structural basis for TCR-pMHC binding. Quantifying Cross-Attention Interaction in Transformers for Interpreting TCR-pMHC Binding The Fourteenth International Conference on Learning Representations ICLR Jiarui Li Zixiang Yin Haley Smith Zhengming Ding Samuel J. Landry Ramgopal R. Mettu
desc: The Fourteenth International Conference on Learning Representations

title: Rational Multi-Modal Transformers for TCR-pMHC Prediction
content: T cell receptor (TCR) recognition of peptide-MHC (pMHC) complexes is central to immunity and T cell-based therapies. We introduce an explanation-driven framework that uses a new post-hoc analysis method to guide the design of a novel encoder-decoder transformer for TCR-pMHC prediction. By revealing the most informative TCR-epitope features, our method optimizes cross-attention design, auxiliary objectives, and an explanation-based early-stopping strategy. The resulting model achieves state-of-the-art accuracy with improved robustness and interpretability, offering new insight into sequence-level binding mechanisms. The overall pipline of development of a multi-modal transformer model for TCR-pMHC binding prediction. Rational Multi-Modal Transformers for TCR-pMHC Prediction The ACM Conference on Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, and Health Informatics ACM BCB Jiarui Li Zixiang Yin Zhengming Ding Samuel J. Landry Ramgopal R. Mettu
desc: The ACM Conference on Bioinformatics, Computational Biology, and Health Informatics

title: GPU Acceleration of Conformational Stability Computation for CD4+ T-cell Epitope Prediction
content: CD4+ T cells play a crucial role in adaptive immunity and are a significant component of immunological response in many settings. Computational prediction of which antigenic peptides are presented and bind to T cells is a problem that has been studied for several decades. Current efforts apply supervised learning methods to predict peptide-MHCII binding, but do not incorporate the role of antigen processing. To address this, our group developed the Antigen Processing Likelihood (APL) algorithm, which relies on a free energy-based conformational stability metric known as COREX. COREX requires the analysis of a potentially large conformational ensemble and is thus computationally intensive. In recent prior work, we parallelized this analysis with an algorithm we called pCOREX. pCOREX reduced the computation time from hours/days to minutes, demonstrating a near-ideal speedup on 192 CPU cores. In this paper, we achieve an even more substantial acceleration of the COREX algorithm by making use of GPU cores, and demonstrate a reduction in computation time from minutes to seconds The GPUCOREX can be installed use command: shell pip install gpucorex @ git+https://github.com/Jiarui0923/gpuCOREX@0.0.2 GPU Acceleration of Conformational Stability Computation for CD4+ T-cell Epitope Prediction International Conference on Bioinformatics and Biomedicine BIBM Jiarui Li Samuel J. Landry Ramgopal R. Mettu
desc: International Conference on Bioinformatics and Biomedicine

title: CMPS6790 Final Project
content: 📓 Read Jupyter Notebook for This Project T Cell Receptor Modeling 25-11-2024 21:19:05 - T Cell Receptor Modeling - Introduction - Background - Antigen Processsing \& Presenting - T Cell \& T Cell Receptor - Problem Define \& Motivation - More Links \& Guidelines - Environment And Packages - Dataset - VDJdb - Dataset Introduction - Reason for Choose This Dataset - NetMHCII Dataset - Dataset Introduction - Reason for Choose This Dataset - Questions for These Dataset - (ETL) Extraction, Transform, and Load - Extract - VDJdb - NetMHCII Dataset - Transform - Filter Columns - Example - Filter TRB - Example - Clean N/A Data - Example - Filter High Quality Data - Example - Intigrate The MHC Representation - Example - Merge Dataset - Example - Load - Analysis - Stat Species - Analyze V/J Region Relationship - Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (V Region) - The Positive Sample Number - The Sample Distribution - Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (J Region) - The Positive Sample Number - The Sample Distribution - Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (CDR3 Region) - The Positive Sample Number - The Sample Distribution - Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (Epitope Region) - The Positive Sample Number - The Sample Distribution - Model - Candidate Models \& Parameters - Metrics - Model I/O Data Prepare - Only CDR3 Data - Metrics - Parameters - Confusion Matrices - Only Antigen Data - Metrics - Parameters - Confusion Matrices - Only Antigen Data - Metrics - Parameters - Confusion Matrices - Model Result Analyze - Model Interpret - Insight \& Policy Decision Introduction - Author : Jiarui Li - Email : jli78@tulane.edu - Github Page Link : https://cmps6790.jiarui-li.com/ - Course : CMPS 6790 Data Science - Course Link : https://nmattei.github.io/cmps6790/projects/FinalTutorial/FinalTutorial/ This is the coursework for the final deliverable of CMPS 6790 Data Science. For this project, we will leverage data science tools to collect, process, and analyze the TCR data. Based on this, we will build machine learning models and find out the inference procedure. The final work will be uploaded to my Github page. Background Immune system is the guardiance of human-being bodies to protect us from antigents that causes diseases such as virus, cancer cells, and small germs. T cells play a crucial role in adaptive immunity and are a significant component of immunological response in many settings. Therefore, identifying how the CD4+ T cell is activated and starting immune response is important for vaccine and immuno-therapy development. According to the existed researches, generally, the T cell immune reponse pathway consists of several steps $^{[1]}$. Antigen Processsing & Presenting Before being recognized by T cell, the antigens are firstly processed and presented by antigen processing cells. There are generally two types of MHC pathways: MHC-I for intracelluler antigents and MHC-II for extracelluler entigens. These molecules will bind to the APC-processed antigen proteins. Then, the MHC-peptide complex will be presented on the surface of APCs $^{[2,3]}$. T Cell & T Cell Receptor The TCR is consists of $\alpha$ and $\beta$ two chains. And each complementarity determining region (CDR) consist of variable (V), joining (J), and diversity (D) gene segments. The combinations of the variants of these segments leading to diverse of CDRs. Therefore, predicting whether the TCR and MHC-peptide will be binded together is a cutting-edge problem $^{[4]}$. Problem Define & Motivation This problem could be defined as a computer science style question. The input is the sequence/structure of MHC-peptide and $\alpha\beta$ CDRs. The expected output is whether they will be binded together (Binary Classification). During exploring this problem, researchers have collected a plenty of data. Therefore, it is the best choice to use Data Science tool to identify the possible rules behind the data. $^{[1]}$ Marshall, J. S., Warrington, R., Watson, W., & Kim, H. L. (2018). An introduction to immunology and immunopathology. Allergy, Asthma & Clinical Immunology, 14, 1-10. $^{[2]}$ Leone, P., Shin, E. C., Perosa, F., Vacca, A., Dammacco, F., & Racanelli, V. (2013). MHC class I antigen processing and presenting machinery: organization, function, and defects in tumor cells. Journal of the National Cancer Institute, 105(16), 1172-1187. $^{[3]}$ Roche, P. A., & Furuta, K. (2015). The ins and outs of MHC class II-mediated antigen processing and presentation. Nature Reviews Immunology, 15(4), 203-216. $^{[4]}$ Shah, K., Al-Haidari, A., Sun, J., & Kazi, J. U. (2021). T cell receptor (TCR) signaling in health and disease. Signal transduction and targeted therapy, 6(1), 412. More Links & Guidelines There are more links and references for background. 1. T Cell: https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/body/24630-t-cells 2. TCR: https://www.thermofisher.com/us/en/home/life-science/sequencing/sequencing-learning-center/next-generation-sequencing-information/immuno-oncology-research/why-immune-repertoire-matters.html 3. MHC: https://www.britannica.com/science/major-histocompatibility-complex Environment And Packages Environment and packages used for this project is documented below: Name Version Install Description ---: :----------- :---------- :-------------------------------- :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 0 pandas 1.5.3 pip install pandas==1.5.3 pandas - a powerful data analysis and manipulation library for Python ===================================================================== pandas is a Python package providing fast, flexible, a... 1 numpy 1.24.4 pip install numpy==1.24.4 NumPy ===== Provides 1. An array object of arbitrary homogeneous items 2. Fast mathematical operations over arrays 3. Linear Algebra, Fourier Transforms, Random Number Generation How to use t... 2 matplotlib 3.7.2 pip install matplotlib==3.7.2 An object-oriented plotting library. A procedural interface is provided by the companion pyplot module, which may be imported directly, e.g.:: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt or using ipython:... 3 requests 2.28.2 pip install requests==2.28.2 Requests HTTP Library Requests is an HTTP library, written in Python, for human beings. Basic GET usage: import requests r = requests.get('https://www.python.org... 4 json 2.0.9 pip install json==2.0.9 JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a subset of JavaScript syntax (ECMA-262 3rd edition) used as a lightweight data interchange format. :mod: json exposes an API familiar to users... 5 bs4 4.12.0 pip install bs4==4.12.0 Beautiful Soup Elixir and Tonic - "The Screen-Scraper's Friend". http://www.crummy.com/software/BeautifulSoup/ Beautiful Soup uses a pluggable XML or HTML parser to parse a (possibly invalid) docume... 6 sklearn 1.3.2 pip install sklearn==1.3.2 Machine learning module for Python ================================== sklearn is a Python module integrating classical machine learning algorithms in the tightly-knit world of scientific Python pack... 7 scipy 1.10.1 pip install scipy==1.10.1 SciPy: A scientific computing package for Python ================================================ Documentation is available in the docstrings and online at https://docs.scipy.org. Contents -------... Dataset VDJdb Dataset Link: https://vdjdb.cdr3.net/ Dataset Introduction VDJdb is a curated database of T-cell receptor (TCR) sequences with known antigen specificities. The primary goal of VDJdb is to facilitate access to existing information on T-cell receptor antigen specificities. Reason for Choose This Dataset T Cell is a significant part of our immune system to protect our body away from cancer, virus, and other antigens. Therefore, Identify the rule that how T Cells are activated can help us understand how to develop vaccine for diseases. To understand how T cell work, T cell receptor (TCR) is the key factor. Therefore, we choose this dataset and try to model TCR. NetMHCII Dataset Dataset Link: http://tools.iedb.org/mhcii/download/ Dataset Introduction This MHC class II binding affinity dataset consists of 44, 541 experimentally measured binging affinities covering 26 alleric variants. Reason for Choose This Dataset The VDJdb only contains positive data, that the peptide-MHC molecule can be binded by the TCR. Therefore, we need to introduce negative data from this dataset. Questions for These Dataset - Which TCR fragement is strongly related to which epitope (processed antigens) fragments? - Is there relationship between different TCR? (For example, is there fragment leads to other fragment appearance.) (ETL) Extraction, Transform, and Load Extract Following the document of VDJdb (https://vdjdb-web.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html), we used requests package to fetch the entire VDJdb database. Then transfer it to dataframe. Analyzing the NetMHCII data page (https://services.healthtech.dtu.dk/suppl/immunology/NAR NetMHCpan NetMHCIIpan/), we used requests and beautifulsoup packages to analyze and extract data from the pages. Then transfer it to dataframe. VDJdb - Data URL : https://vdjdb.cdr3.net/api/database/ - Number of Records : 102990 - Number of Columns : 16 NetMHCII Dataset - Data URL : https://services.healthtech.dtu.dk/suppl/immunology/NAR NetMHCpan NetMHCIIpan/ - Number of Records : 946141 - Number of Columns : 3 Transform Filter Columns Filter the columns we are interested in and drop other columns. - gene : TCR is comprised of two sequence, this denotes which sequence it is. - crd3 : The TCR sequence. - v.segm : The V region of the TCR sequence. - j.segm : The J region of the TCR sequence. - antigen.epitope : The epitope this TCR can bind to. - mhc.a : The MHC sequence of the accepted presented epitope. - species : Species for this record. - vdjdb.score : Experiment confidence. (Can be considered as data quality) Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :---------------- :------------ :----------- :------------------ :------------ :------------ --------------: 10 TRA CAVRDGGTGFQKLVF TRAV3 01 TRAJ8 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 11 TRB CASRQDRDYQETQYF TRBV5-1 01 TRBJ2-5 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 12 TRA CAARGIGSGTYKYIF TRAV13-1 01 TRAJ40 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 13 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 14 TRA CAARGIGSGTYKYIF TRAV13-1 01 TRAJ40 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Filter TRB According to the typical researches, we commonly focus on TRB, which is significantly decided the behaviour of the TCR. Therefore, we only keep records for TRB. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Clean N/A Data Clean all N/A data for columns v.segm and j.segm Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Filter High Quality Data vdjdb.score shows confidence of this data. Therefore, to make sure our analysis depends on high quality data. We only keep records with vdjdb.score = 1. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Intigrate The MHC Representation We could observe that the way NetMHCII and VDJdb used to represent MHC are different. Therefore, we need to make them the same. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :----------- :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Merge Dataset Finally, we could join NetMHCII dataset to VDJdb to add negative samples to the dataset. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score bind ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :----------- :------------ --------------: -------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:08 HomoSapiens 1 1 Load We now use CSV file to manage the data. Therefore, we store back the preprocessed data to a CSV file. When we need it, we could use pandas to read it. Read file example Unnamed: 0 gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score bind ---: -------------: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :----------- :------------ --------------: -------: 0 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 2 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 3 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 4 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:08 HomoSapiens 1 1 Analysis Stat Species Because for different species, the patterns of TCR might be different. We are going to know how many species are there and the distribution of the species. According to the analysis, we could see, most of the samples are from Homo Spanies. species :-------------- ----------: HomoSapiens 105248 MusMusculus 3347 MacacaMulatta 2032 Visualization Analyze V/J Region Relationship V and J regions significantly decided the function of the TCR. Therefore, it is interesting to analyze their distribution. We could see, most of the samples are produced from the specified V and J regions. That means, our model needs to be able to process this imbalance. Visualization Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (V Region) Analyze the distribution of positive or negative samples based on V region. It is obvious that, the distribution of positive and negative samples are different by different V regions. Visualization The Positive Sample Number The Sample Distribution Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (J Region) Analyze the distribution of positive or negative samples based on J region. The J regions is more balanced comparing to V regions. Visualization The Positive Sample Number The Sample Distribution Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (CDR3 Region) Analyze the distribution of positive or negative samples based on CDR3 sequence. It shows significantly different for positve and negative distribution. Visualization The Positive Sample Number The Sample Distribution Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (Epitope Region) Analyze the distribution of positive or negative samples based on Epitope sequence. Visualization The Positive Sample Number The Sample Distribution Model The analysis shows that some sequence represents higher probability to affect the binding status. Also, the data shows significant bias and long tail. Therefore, we are going to use two kinds of different models: 1. Decision Tree Based Models e.g. Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost These models can be used to predict the binding status, also, can show us how them inference results. Also, it has good ability for analyze unbalanced data. The V-J relationship diagram, the data is imbalanced, but the distribution shows there are different probability that different sequence and class leads to different epitope binding probability. 2. Clustering Models e.g. k-means We could also try clustering, which allows us to inference from the distance of the sequence units to the binding status. 3. Linear Regressions e.g. Ridge, Linear It is also possible there is linear relationship, therefore, we will also try these models. Candidate Models & Parameters We will test these models below to find the best model and parameter combination. SVC - kernel : ('linear', 'rbf') - C : [1, 10, 100, 1000] DecisionTree - min samples split : [2, 3, 4] - min samples leaf : [1, 5, 10] - criterion : ['gini', 'entropy'] RandomForest - n estimators : [50, 100, 250] - min samples leaf : [1, 5, 10] - criterion : ['gini', 'entropy'] GradientBoosting - n estimators : [50, 100, 250] - min samples leaf : [1, 5, 10] - criterion : ['friedman mse', 'squared error'] MLP - hidden layer sizes : [(64,), (64, 64), (64, 64, 64)] - activation : ['relu', 'logistic'] KNeighbors - n neighbors : [5, 10, 100] - weights : ['uniform', 'distance'] Ridge - tol : [1e-05, 0.0001, 0.001] - alpha : [1, 0.1, 0.01] SGD - loss : ['hinge', 'squared error', 'log loss'] - tol : [0.0001, 0.001, 0.01] KMeans - n clusters : [5, 10, 15, 50, 100] Metrics Accuracy python 'Accuracy': lambda y, y : metrics.accuracy score(y, y ), Precision python 'Precision': lambda y, y : metrics.precision score(y, y , zero division=0, average='weighted'), Recall python 'Recall': lambda y, y : metrics.recall score(y, y , zero division=0, average='weighted'), F1 python 'F1': lambda y, y : metrics.f1 score(y, y , zero division=0, average='weighted'), P-Value(T) python 'P-Value(T)':lambda y, y : stats.ttest rel(y, y )[1], T-Test python 'T-Test': lambda y, y : stats.ttest rel(y, y )[0], Pearson-R python 'Pearson-R': lambda y, y : stats.pearsonr(y, y )[0], Model I/O Data Prepare Due to the data is categorical, we will transform it to be computable. Also, to save time, we will use just partial of our data to search the best model and parameters. Only CDR3 Data We first only use CDR3 data to evaluate the models. Use 5-folds to search parameter and 10-folds to validate the models. Metrics Accuracy Precision Recall F1 P-Value(T) T-Test Pearson-R :----------------- -----------: ------------: ---------: ----------: -------------: -----------: ------------: SVC 0.574 0.575826 0.574 0.573907 0.599077 -0.158217 0.137301 DecisionTree 0.521 0.529892 0.521 0.521812 0.318524 0.424652 0.0431772 RandomForest 0.522 0.52616 0.522 0.521037 0.45438 -0.625927 0.035842 GradientBoosting 0.525 0.526318 0.525 0.523883 0.495046 -0.570352 0.0361783 MLP 0.549 0.555267 0.549 0.548436 0.271902 -0.148985 0.0952885 KNeighbors 0.558 0.56508 0.558 0.554874 0.424499 -1.01628 0.112773 Ridge 0.529 0.532499 0.529 0.522757 0.351262 -1.37116 0.0456602 SGD 0.562 0.571228 0.562 0.542092 0.118912 -0.470258 0.117239 KMeans 0.012 0.2535 0.012 0.0228156 1.13267e-24 -16.6762 0.0744671 Parameters C kernel :---- ----: :--------- SVC 10 rbf criterion min samples leaf min samples split :------------- :------------ -------------------: --------------------: DecisionTree entropy 1 2 criterion min samples leaf n estimators :------------- :------------ -------------------: ---------------: RandomForest entropy 1 50 criterion min samples leaf n estimators :----------------- :------------- -------------------: ---------------: GradientBoosting friedman mse 10 100 activation hidden layer sizes :---- :------------- :--------------------- MLP relu (64, 64) n neighbors weights :----------- --------------: :---------- KNeighbors 100 distance alpha tol :------ --------: ------: Ridge 1 0.001 loss tol :---- :------- -------: SGD hinge 0.0001 n clusters :------- -------------: KMeans 100 Confusion Matrices Only Antigen Data We then only use Antigen data to evaluate the models. Use 5-folds to search parameter and 10-folds to validate the models. Metrics Accuracy Precision Recall F1 P-Value(T) T-Test Pearson-R :----------------- -----------: ------------: ---------: ----------: -------------: ------------: ------------: SVC 0.973 0.973939 0.973 0.97303 0.389533 -0.181498 0.94617 DecisionTree 0.961 0.962777 0.961 0.961035 0.226342 0.497921 0.922818 RandomForest 0.975 0.975972 0.975 0.975002 0.289226 -0.637664 0.950197 GradientBoosting 0.973 0.974121 0.973 0.973018 0.263006 -0.185661 0.94624 MLP 0.976 0.97678 0.976 0.975998 0.367078 -0.371899 0.952148 KNeighbors 0.96 0.960573 0.96 0.959994 0.489511 0.00242382 0.91903 Ridge 0.906 0.913855 0.906 0.906151 0.201093 1.783 0.817481 SGD 0.896 0.905593 0.896 0.896156 0.245403 1.89513 0.799134 KMeans 0.049 0.346 0.049 0.0805845 3.5364e-14 -9.29764 0.136392 Parameters C kernel :---- ----: :--------- SVC 10 rbf criterion min samples leaf min samples split :------------- :------------ -------------------: --------------------: DecisionTree entropy 1 2 criterion min samples leaf n estimators :------------- :------------ -------------------: ---------------: RandomForest gini 1 250 criterion min samples leaf n estimators :----------------- :------------- -------------------: ---------------: GradientBoosting friedman mse 10 250 activation hidden layer sizes :---- :------------- :--------------------- MLP relu (64, 64, 64) n neighbors weights :----------- --------------: :---------- KNeighbors 5 distance alpha tol :------ --------: ------: Ridge 0.1 1e-05 loss tol :---- :------- ------: SGD hinge 0.01 n clusters :------- -------------: KMeans 100 Confusion Matrices Only Antigen Data We could observed that, if we only use CDR3, the results seem not that good. But if we only use Antigen data, the accuracy seems very high. According to this, could we get better performance with mixed two features together. Metrics Accuracy Precision Recall F1 P-Value(T) T-Test Pearson-R :----------------- -----------: ------------: ---------: ----------: -------------: ------------: ------------: SVC 0.962 0.96284 0.962 0.961985 0.299863 -0.133562 0.923511 DecisionTree 0.956 0.95724 0.956 0.956065 0.337478 0.288011 0.91199 RandomForest 0.972 0.973144 0.972 0.972003 0.431874 -0.93781 0.944213 GradientBoosting 0.975 0.975609 0.975 0.975019 0.355486 -0.170541 0.949897 MLP 0.938 0.939429 0.938 0.938053 0.570777 0.415939 0.8756 KNeighbors 0.923 0.925425 0.923 0.923105 0.389357 1.08884 0.845832 Ridge 0.9 0.907395 0.9 0.90022 0.170988 1.74889 0.804842 SGD 0.852 0.868348 0.852 0.849793 0.075584 -0.0450871 0.714594 KMeans 0.017 0.323333 0.017 0.0320237 1.72765e-25 -16.118 0.0850889 Parameters C kernel :---- ----: :--------- SVC 10 rbf criterion min samples leaf min samples split :------------- :------------ -------------------: --------------------: DecisionTree gini 1 3 criterion min samples leaf n estimators :------------- :------------ -------------------: ---------------: RandomForest gini 1 250 criterion min samples leaf n estimators :----------------- :------------- -------------------: ---------------: GradientBoosting friedman mse 1 250 activation hidden layer sizes :---- :------------- :--------------------- MLP relu (64, 64, 64) n neighbors weights :----------- --------------: :---------- KNeighbors 5 uniform alpha tol :------ --------: ------: Ridge 0.01 1e-05 loss tol :---- :--------- ------: SGD log loss 0.01 n clusters :------- -------------: KMeans 100 Confusion Matrices Model Result Analyze We could observe that, joint of CDR3 and antigen data decreased the performance. Therefore, antigen sequence contributes more to the prediction. Model Interpret We could visualize the importance of the different units in CDR3 and antigens. We could see, the antigen plays more important role in model prediction. Also, in antigens, G , L , S , and T play more important role, and in CDR3, F , L , and S play more important role. Insight & Policy Decision According to the analyze, we could conclude that, antigen sequence plays more significant role in T Cell and MHC-peptide binding prediction procedure. The CDR3 sequence plays negative influences. With the model analysis results, we could see, Random Forest achieves the best performance. The visualization of the feature importance of Random Forest could also prove our analysis is correct. Also, based on the importance analysis, we could see in antigens, G, L, S, and T play more important role, and in CDR3, F, L, and S play more important role. Therefore, in later T Cell research, we could prior to use Random Forest to modeling the data. And, pay more attention to the antigen sequence with high frequency of G, L, S, and T units.
desc: Final project: T Cell Receptor modeling for CMPS 6790 Data Science.

title: CMPS6790 Milestone2
content: 📓 Read Jupyter Notebook for This Project T Cell Receptor Modeling 24-10-2024 13:14:07 - T Cell Receptor Modeling - Introduction - Environment And Packages - Dataset - VDJdb - Dataset Introduction - Reason for Choose This Dataset - NetMHCII Dataset - Dataset Introduction - Reason for Choose This Dataset - Questions for These Dataset - (ETL) Extraction, Transform, and Load - Extract - VDJdb - NetMHCII Dataset - Transform - Filter Columns - Example - Filter TRB - Example - Clean N/A Data - Example - Filter High Quality Data - Example - Intigrate The MHC Representation - Example - Merge Dataset - Example - Load - Analysis - Stat Species - Analyze V/J Region Relationship - Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (J Region) - The Positive Sample Number - The Sample Distribution - Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (J Region) - The Positive Sample Number - The Sample Distribution - Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (CDR3 Region) - The Positive Sample Number - The Sample Distribution - Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (Epitope Region) - The Positive Sample Number - The Sample Distribution - Model Introduction - Author : Jiarui Li - Email : jli78@tulane.edu - Github Page Link : https://www.jiarui-li.com/CMPS6790/ - Course : CMPS 6790 Data Science - Course Link : https://nmattei.github.io/cmps6790/projects/FinalTutorial/FinalTutorial/ This is the coursework for the Milestone 02 of CMPS 6790 Data Science. For this project, I will merge one more related dataset NetMHCII Dataset , have more ETA analysis, and propose prediction models. The final work will be uploaded to my Github page. Environment And Packages Environment and packages used for this project is documented below: Name Version Install Description ---: :----------- :---------- :-------------------------------- :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 0 pandas 2.2.3 pip install pandas==2.2.3 pandas - a powerful data analysis and manipulation library for Python ===================================================================== pandas is a Python package providing fast, flexible, a... 1 numpy 2.0.0 pip install numpy==2.0.0 NumPy ===== Provides 1. An array object of arbitrary homogeneous items 2. Fast mathematical operations over arrays 3. Linear Algebra, Fourier Transforms, Random Number Generation How to use t... 2 matplotlib 3.9.2 pip install matplotlib==3.9.2 An object-oriented plotting library. A procedural interface is provided by the companion pyplot module, which may be imported directly, e.g.:: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt or using ipython:... 3 requests 2.32.3 pip install requests==2.32.3 Requests HTTP Library Requests is an HTTP library, written in Python, for human beings. Basic GET usage: import requests r = requests.get('https://www.python.org... 4 json 2.0.9 pip install json==2.0.9 JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a subset of JavaScript syntax (ECMA-262 3rd edition) used as a lightweight data interchange format. :mod: json exposes an API familiar to user... 5 bs4 4.12.3 pip install bs4==4.12.3 Beautiful Soup Elixir and Tonic - "The Screen-Scraper's Friend". http://www.crummy.com/software/BeautifulSoup/ Beautiful Soup uses a pluggable XML or HTML parser to parse a (possibly invalid) docume... Dataset VDJdb Dataset Link: https://vdjdb.cdr3.net/ Dataset Introduction VDJdb is a curated database of T-cell receptor (TCR) sequences with known antigen specificities. The primary goal of VDJdb is to facilitate access to existing information on T-cell receptor antigen specificities. Reason for Choose This Dataset T Cell is a significant part of our immune system to protect our body away from cancer, virus, and other antigens. Therefore, Identify the rule that how T Cells are activated can help us understand how to develop vaccine for diseases. To understand how T cell work, T cell receptor (TCR) is the key factor. Therefore, we choose this dataset and try to model TCR. NetMHCII Dataset Dataset Link: http://tools.iedb.org/mhcii/download/ Dataset Introduction This MHC class II binding affinity dataset consists of 44, 541 experimentally measured binging affinities covering 26 alleric variants. Reason for Choose This Dataset The VDJdb only contains positive data, that the peptide-MHC molecule can be binded by the TCR. Therefore, we need to introduce negative data from this dataset. Questions for These Dataset - Which TCR fragement is strongly related to which epitope (processed antigens) fragments? - Is there relationship between different TCR? (For example, is there fragment leads to other fragment appearance.) (ETL) Extraction, Transform, and Load Extract Following the document of VDJdb (https://vdjdb-web.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html), we used requests package to fetch the entire VDJdb database. Then transfer it to dataframe. Analyzing the NetMHCII data page (https://services.healthtech.dtu.dk/suppl/immunology/NAR NetMHCpan NetMHCIIpan/), we used requests and beautifulsoup packages to analyze and extract data from the pages. Then transfer it to dataframe. VDJdb - Data URL : https://vdjdb.cdr3.net/api/database/ - Number of Records : 102990 - Number of Columns : 16 NetMHCII Dataset - Data URL : https://services.healthtech.dtu.dk/suppl/immunology/NAR NetMHCpan NetMHCIIpan/ - Number of Records : 946141 - Number of Columns : 3 Transform Filter Columns Filter the columns we are interested in and drop other columns. - gene : TCR is comprised of two sequence, this denotes which sequence it is. - crd3 : The TCR sequence. - v.segm : The V region of the TCR sequence. - j.segm : The J region of the TCR sequence. - antigen.epitope : The epitope this TCR can bind to. - mhc.a : The MHC sequence of the accepted presented epitope. - species : Species for this record. - vdjdb.score : Experiment confidence. (Can be considered as data quality) Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :---------------- :------------ :----------- :------------------ :------------ :------------ --------------: 10 TRA CAVRDGGTGFQKLVF TRAV3 01 TRAJ8 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 11 TRB CASRQDRDYQETQYF TRBV5-1 01 TRBJ2-5 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 12 TRA CAARGIGSGTYKYIF TRAV13-1 01 TRAJ40 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 13 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 14 TRA CAARGIGSGTYKYIF TRAV13-1 01 TRAJ40 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Filter TRB According to the typical researches, we commonly focus on TRB, which is significantly decided the behaviour of the TCR. Therefore, we only keep records for TRB. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Clean N/A Data Clean all N/A data for columns v.segm and j.segm Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Filter High Quality Data vdjdb.score shows confidence of this data. Therefore, to make sure our analysis depends on high quality data. We only keep records with vdjdb.score = 1. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:01 HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B 35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Intigrate The MHC Representation We could observe that the way NetMHCII and VDJdb used to represent MHC are different. Therefore, we need to make them the same. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :----------- :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:08 HomoSapiens 1 Merge Dataset Finally, we could join NetMHCII dataset to VDJdb to add negative samples to the dataset. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score bind ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :----------- :------------ --------------: -------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:08 HomoSapiens 1 1 Load We now use CSV file to manage the data. Therefore, we store back the preprocessed data to a CSV file. When we need it, we could use pandas to read it. Read file example Unnamed: 0 gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope mhc.a species vdjdb.score bind ---: -------------: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :----------- :------------ --------------: -------: 0 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 2 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 3 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:01 HomoSapiens 1 1 4 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HLA-B35:08 HomoSapiens 1 1 Analysis Stat Species Because for different species, the patterns of TCR might be different. We are going to know how many species are there and the distribution of the species. species count :-------------- --------: HomoSapiens 120881 MusMusculus 3371 MacacaMulatta 2032 Visualization Analyze V/J Region Relationship V and J regions significantly decided the function of the TCR. Therefore, it is interesting to analyze their distribution. Visualization Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (J Region) Analyze the distribution of positive or negative samples based on V region Visualization The Positive Sample Number The Sample Distribution Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (J Region) Analyze the distribution of positive or negative samples based on J region Visualization The Positive Sample Number The Sample Distribution Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (CDR3 Region) Analyze the distribution of positive or negative samples based on CDR3 sequence. Visualization The Positive Sample Number The Sample Distribution Analyze The Positive/Negative Sample Distribution (Epitope Region) Analyze the distribution of positive or negative samples based on Epitope sequence. Visualization The Positive Sample Number The Sample Distribution Model The analysis shows that some sequence represents higher probability to affect the binding status. Also, the data shows significant bias and long tail. Therefore, we are going to use two kinds of different models: 1. Decision Tree Based Models e.g. Decision Tree, Random Forest, XGBoost These models can be used to predict the binding status, also, can show us how them inference results. Also, it has good ability for analyze unbalanced data. The V-J relationship diagram, the data is imbalanced, but the distribution shows there are different probability that different sequence and class leads to different epitope binding probability. 2. Bayesian Inference We could also try bayesian inference, which allows us to inference from the combination of the sequence units to the binding status. Due to the sample number distribution hist diagram, the probability of different sequences that leads to bind or non-bind are different.

title: CMPS6790
content: CMPS6790 Data Science - Author : Jiarui Li - Email : jli78@tulane.edu - Github Page Link : https://jiarui-li.com/ - Course : CMPS 6790 Data Science - Course Link : https://nmattei.github.io/cmps6790/projects/FinalTutorial/FinalTutorial/ Course Projects - Milestone 01: https://CMPS6790-Milestone1.jiarui-li.com - Milestone 02: https://CMPS6790-Milestone2.jiarui-li.com - Final Project (Deliverable 01): https://CMPS6790-Final.jiarui-li.com

title: GPU Acceleration for Markov Chain Monte Carlo Sampling
content: In areas such as molecular biology, computer vision, and natural language processing, graphs are commonly used to represent the structure of probability distributions (or their equivalents) that are too large to consider explicitly. In these settings, we are commonly interested in sampling from the distribution for various inference tasks. A typical approach is the use of Markov Chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) methods. The classic, uniprocessor, approach to MCMC still results in poor performance. While parallel approaches on CPU or GPU devices have been proposed, they are often designed for specific tasks and/or do not effectively utilize the inter-device communication capabilities of GPUs. We propose a novel, GPU-parallelizable MCMC method for this setting. Our approach makes use of a partitioning approach to divide the graph and dispatch the resulting sub-graphs to GPUs. Using the communication capabilities of GPUs (e.g., NVLink), we give a way to coordinate information between the computations over adjacent subgraphs and subsequently merge them. This approach takes advantage of the high degree of GPU parallelism while maintaining the generality of MCMC sampling. We demonstrate the performance of our approach for estimating protein conformational stability. Over four different benchmarks and two GPU platforms we show that our method achieves up to a 400% speedup over an adaptive Monte Carlo sampling method. Schematic of our approach. GPU Acceleration for Markov Chain Monte Carlo Sampling Proceedings of the 4th International Conference on AI-ML Systems AIMLSys Jiarui Li Samuel J. Landry Ramgopal R. Mettu
desc: Proceedings of the 4th International Conference on AI-ML Systems

title: CMPS6790 Milestone1
content:
📓 Read Jupyter Notebook for This Project T Cell Receptor Modeling Based on VDJdb 30-09-2024 13:07:44 - T Cell Receptor Modeling Based on VDJdb - Introduction - Workflow And Collaboration Plan - Environment And Packages - Dataset - VDJdb - Dataset Introduction - Reason for Choose This Dataset - Questions for This Dataset - (ETL) Extraction, Transform, and Load - Extract - Transform - Filter Columns - Example - Filter TRB - Example - Clean N/A Data - Example - Filter High Quality Data - Example - Load - Analysis - Stat Species - Analyze V/J Region Relationship Introduction - Author : Jiarui Li - Email : jli78@tulane.edu - Github Page Link : https://jiarui-li.com/ - Course : CMPS 6790 Data Science - Course Link : https://nmattei.github.io/cmps6790/projects/FinalTutorial/FinalTutorial/ This is the coursework for the Milestone 01 of CMPS 6790 Data Science. For this project, I will leverage dataset VDJdb and task T Cell Receptor Modeling to learn the ETL (Extraction, Transform, and Load) skills. The final work will be uploaded to my Github page. Workflow And Collaboration Plan For this project, it is finished independently by Jiarui Li. Therefore, this section will mainly describe the workflow. 1. Code and version management The code will be managed in a git repository to manage the version and sync all updates. 2. Code organization For submit convenience, all code will be in one jupyter notebook. Also, no extra files required. The package requirement will be attached to this notebook. 3. Document organization The document will be managed by docflow, which allows us to show everything in jupyter notebook also generate a markdown file for GitHub page. The code used to generate the page is at below. 4. Development procedure This poject development will follow the protocal of rapid application development cycle. First, we will decide the developement problem and target. Then, we will plan the TODO list for currect cycle. Finally, develop the codes, test them, and identify the new issues and requirement. python def generate github page(doc dict:dict, title:str='', save path:str='page.md') - None: ''' Function used to generate the final github page. Parameters ---------- doc dict (dict[doc.Document]) : The sections of this page. title (str) : The title of this page. save path (str) : The save path to store the html file ''' doc title = doc.Title(title, level=1) Extract sections as list doc sections = [ doc for doc in doc dict.values()] Append title to section list for calalog generation doc sections = [ doc title] + doc sections final doc = doc.Document( doc title, doc.DateTimeStamp('%d-%m-%Y %H:%M:%S'), Add page generation timestamp doc.Text('

'), doc.Catalog(doc.Document( doc sections)), Add catalog for entire document doc.Text('

'), doc sections[1:] Add all sections ) final doc.save(save path, format='markdown') Environment And Packages Environment and packages used for this project is documented below: Name Version Install Description ---: :----------- :---------- :-------------------------------- :------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ 0 pandas 2.0.3 pip install pandas==2.0.3 pandas - a powerful data analysis and manipulation library for Python ===================================================================== pandas is a Python package providing fast, flexible, a... 1 numpy 1.24.4 pip install numpy==1.24.4 NumPy ===== Provides 1. An array object of arbitrary homogeneous items 2. Fast mathematical operations over arrays 3. A procedural interface is provided by the companion pyplot module, which may be imported directly, e.g.:: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt or using ipython:... 3 requests 2.32.3 pip install requests==2.32.3 Requests HTTP Library Requests is an HTTP library, written in Python, for human beings. Basic GET usage: import requests r = requests.get('https://www.python.org... 4 json 2.0.9 pip install json==2.0.9 JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a subset of JavaScript syntax (ECMA-262 3rd edition) used as a lightweight data interchange format. :mod: json exposes an API familiar to users... Dataset VDJdb Dataset Link: https://vdjdb.cdr3.net/ Dataset Introduction VDJdb is a curated database of T-cell receptor (TCR) sequences with known antigen specificities. The primary goal of VDJdb is to facilitate access to existing information on T-cell receptor antigen specificities. Reason for Choose This Dataset T Cell is a significant part of our immune system to protect our body away from cancer, virus, and other antigens. Therefore, Identify the rule that how T Cells are activated can help us understand how to develop vaccine for diseases. To understand how T cell work, T cell receptor (TCR) is the key factor. Therefore, we choose this dataset and try to model TCR. Questions for This Dataset - Which TCR fragement is strongly related to which epitope (processed antigens) fragments? - Is there relationship between different TCR? (For example, is there fragment leads to other fragment appearance.) (ETL) Extraction, Transform, and Load Extract Following the document of VDJdb (https://vdjdb-web.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api.html), we used requests package to fetch the entire VDJdb database. Then transfer it to dataframe. - Data URL : https://vdjdb.cdr3.net/api/database/ - Number of Records : 102990 - Number of Columns : 16 Transform Filter Columns Filter the columns we are interested in and drop other columns. - gene : TCR is comprised of two sequence, this denotes which sequence it is. - crd3 : The TCR sequence. - v.segm : The V region of the TCR sequence. - j.segm : The J region of the TCR sequence. - antigen.epitope : The epitope this TCR can bind to. - species : Species for this record. - vdjdb.score : Experiment confidence. (Can be considered as data quality) Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :---------------- :------------ :----------- :------------------ :------------ --------------: 10 TRA CAVRDGGTGFQKLVF TRAV3 01 TRAJ8 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 11 TRB CASRQDRDYQETQYF TRBV5-1 01 TRBJ2-5 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 12 TRA CAARGIGSGTYKYIF TRAV13-1 01 TRAJ40 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 13 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 14 TRA CAARGIGSGTYKYIF TRAV13-1 01 TRAJ40 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 Filter TRB According to the typical researches, we commonly focus on TRB, which is significantly decided the behaviour of the TCR. Therefore, we only keep records for TRB. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 Clean N/A Data Clean all N/A data for columns v.segm and j.segm Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 Filter High Quality Data vdjdb.score shows confidence of this data. Therefore, to make sure our analysis depends on high quality data. We only keep records with vdjdb.score = 1. Example gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope species vdjdb.score ---: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ --------------: 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 Load We now use CSV file to manage the data. Therefore, we store back the preprocessed data to a CSV file. When we need it, we could use pandas to read it. Read file example Unnamed: 0 gene cdr3 v.segm j.segm antigen.epitope species vdjdb.score ---: -------------: :------- :-------------- :--------- :----------- :------------------ :------------ --------------: 0 1 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 1 3 TRB CASSARSGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 2 5 TRB CASSAPTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 3 7 TRB CASSARTGELFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-2 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 4 8 TRB CASSPRRYNEQFF TRBV9 01 TRBJ2-1 01 HPVGEADYFEY HomoSapiens 1 Analysis Stat Species Because for different species, the patterns of TCR might be different. We are going to know how many species are there and the distribution of the species. species count :-------------- --------: HomoSapiens 8185 MusMusculus 1198 MacacaMulatta 659 Visualization Analyze V/J Region Relationship V and J regions significantly decided the function of the TCR. Therefore, it is interesting to analyze their distribution. Visualization
desc: Milestone 1: T Cell Receptor modeling based on VDJdb.
